meta.description: Gold answers for flt_tasks.json benchmark, derived from flt_concept_graph.json v3.4 by graph traversal

T01.note: All nodes reachable via defined_using from vc_dimension. In the graph, vc_dimension has only one defined_using edge: to shatters. shatters has no outgoing defined_using edges.

T02.note: All nodes X where X characterizes pac_learning, plus fundamental_theorem. meta_pac_bound was reclassified from characterizes to restricts (it is a specialization, not a bidirectional equivalence).

T03.note: does_not_imply edge from pac_learning to mistake_bounded exists with witness about thresholds on R.

T04.note: Four nodes have upper_bounds -> generalization_error. The task expected algorithmic_stability too, but in the graph algorithmic_stability has defined_using -> generalization_error, not upper_bounds.

T05.note: All four edge traversals confirmed in graph: bayesian_learner instance_of learner, pac_bayes_bound upper_bounds generalization_error, natarajan_dimension does_not_imply pac_learning, ds_dimension characterizes pac_learning.

T06.note: Hierarchy: BC > Ex > FIN. bc_learning restricts ex_learning (BC generalizes Ex), ex_learning strictly_stronger finite_learning.

T07.note: Analogy edge concept_drift -> online_learning has obstruction about data model mismatch: drift uses i.i.d. draws per step, online uses adversarial instances.

T08.note: All nodes X with extends_grammar -> vc_dimension.

T09.note: computational_hardness: lower_bounds pac_learning, used_in_proof vc_dimension and sq_dimension, requires_assumption inductive_bias (crypto assumptions), vc_dimension does_not_imply computational_hardness.

T10.note: Topological sort of defined_using subgraph restricted to PAC-related nodes. Multiple valid orderings exist; this is the canonical one from the graph. Key constraint: domain and label first, concept depends on both, concept_class depends on concept, etc.

T11.note: scope_boundary_rl exists with category scope_boundary. Explicitly excluded.

T12.note: 9 does_not_imply + 4 strictly_stronger = 13 separation results, matching the task expectation.

T13.note: 4 edges among the four nodes: star_number characterizes vc_dimension, littlestone_dimension upper_bounds vc_dimension, sq_dimension analogy vc_dimension, sq_dimension does_not_imply vc_dimension.

T14.note: pseudodimension extends_grammar vc_dimension, pseudodimension restricts fat_shattering_dimension. Three nodes extend VC grammar: ordinal_vc_dim, pseudodimension, natarajan_dimension.

T15.note: fundamental_theorem has no direct edges to multiclass nodes. natarajan_dimension does_not_imply pac_learning (finite d_N insufficient when |Y|=inf). ds_dimension characterizes pac_learning. natarajan extends_grammar vc_dimension. ds_dimension strictly_stronger natarajan_dimension.
